IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: IN ISRAEL, WE SUPPORT HEALTHCARE, EDUCATION, YOUTH PROGRAMS. SEE SCHEDULE O.IN ISRAEL, WE SUPPORT HEALTHCARE, EDUCATION, YOUTH PROGRAMS AND LAND DEVELOPMENT. IN THE U.S., ENHANCE THE QUALITY OF AMERICAN AND JEWISH LIFE.
IRS990.MissionDesc: HADASSAH, THE WOMEN'S ZIONIST ORGANIZATION OF AMERICA, INC. (HWZOA) IS A NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION EXEMPT UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE U.S. INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. SEE SCHEDULE O.
IRS990.Desc: IN COMMUNITIES NATIONWIDE, HADASSAH CHAPTERS PRESENT ADVOCACY, EDUCATION, AND COMMUNITY SERVICE INITIATIVES, IN ADDITION TO RAISING AWARENESS ABOUT THE WORK OF HADASSAH MEDICAL ORGANIZATION IN ISRAEL AND ITS EFFORTS TO ADVANCE HEALTH GLOBALLY. SEE SCHEDULE O.

Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: SCHEDULE A, PART II, LINE 10, EXPLANATION OF OTHER INCOME:
Form990ScheduleAPartVIGrp.ExplanationTxt: MISCELLANEOUS CHAPTER ACTIVITY - 2016 AMOUNT: $ 118,597. 2017 AMOUNT: $ 136,097. 2018 AMOUNT: $ 141,531. 2019 AMOUNT: $ 72,813. 2020 AMOUNT: $ 58,279. NET INCOME FROM FUNDRAISING - 2016 AMOUNT: $ 713,666. 2017 AMOUNT: $ 859,808. 2018 AMOUNT: $ 899,620. 2019 AMOUNT: $ 840,086. 2020 AMOUNT: $ 268,715.

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: INCURRED TRAVEL EXPENSES FOR AIRFARE TO A JPAC MEETING. THE MEETING WAS CANCELED & THE CREDIT WILL BE USED TO ATTEND THE NEXT MEETING.
INCURRED EXPENSES FOR DUES PAID TO JPAC (JEWISH PUBLIC AFFAIRS COMMITTEE) OF CALIFORNIA A LOBBYING ORGANIZATION.

HADASSAH, GROUP RETURN IS PREPARED BASED ON INFORMATION PROVIDED FROM APPROXIMATELY 525 UNITS AROUND THE COUNTRY. SOME OF THE INFORMATION REGARDING RAFFLES AND GAMING PROVIDED BY EACH CHAPTER ARE NOT ALWAYS AVAILABLE AT THE TIME OF FILING THE GROUP RETURN. HOWEVER, THE ORGANIZATION REPORTED THE INFORMATION TO THE BEST OF THEIR KNOWLEDGE AND WITH INFORMATION THAT IS READILY AVAILABLE. ONLY A SMALL PERCENTAGE OF THE ORGANIZATION'S CHAPTERS ENGAGE IN RAFFLING.

HADASSAH, THE WOMEN'S ZIONIST ORGANIZATION OF AMERICA, INC. (HWZOA) IS A NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATION EXEMPT UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE U.S. INTERNAL REVENUE CODE. HADASSAH CHAPTERS SUPPORT THE HADASSAH MEDICAL ORGANIZATION AND YOUTH AND EDUCATIONAL INSTITUTIONS IN ISRAEL. IN THE U.S., HADASSAH CHAPTERS PROMOTE WOMEN'S HEALTH EDUCATION, COMMUNITY VOLUNTEERISM, SOCIAL ACTION, JEWISH EDUCATION AND YOUNG JUDAEA YOUTH MOVEMENT.

IN COMMUNITIES NATIONWIDE, HADASSAH CHAPTERS PRESENT ADVOCACY, EDUCATION, AND COMMUNITY SERVICE INITIATIVES, IN ADDITION TO RAISING AWARENESS ABOUT THE WORK OF HADASSAH MEDICAL ORGANIZATION IN ISRAEL AND ITS EFFORTS TO ADVANCE HEALTH GLOBALLY. HADASSAH CHAPTER LEADERS ORGANIZE EDUCATIONAL EVENTS, INCLUDING WOMEN'S HEALTH SEMINARS, SPEAKER AND PANEL PROGRAMS, DISCUSSION GROUPS ABOUT ZIONISM AND ISRAELI, PALESTINIAN RELATIONS, BOOK CLUBS, MOVIE SCREENINGS, PROFESSIONAL GROUPS, AND MORE. HADASSAH CHAPTER MEMBERS ALSO HELP TO SHAPE PUBLIC POLICY THROUGH ADVOCACY ON ISSUES INCLUDING U.S. ISRAEL RELATIONS, ANTI-SEMITISM, WOMEN'S HEALTH, AND HUMAN TRAFFICKING. HADASSAH CHAPTERS GIVE BACK TO THEIR COMMUNITIES, INCLUDING BY PROVIDING BREAST CANCER EDUCATION TO STUDENTS; PRESENTING EDUCATIONAL EVENTS TO THE PUBLIC; AND HOSTING BLOOD, FOOD, AND OTHER DONATION DRIVES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: HADASSAH, THE WOMEN'S ZIONIST ORGANIZATION OF AMERICA, INC. (HADASSAH) IS A VOLUNTEER AND A MEMBERSHIP WOMEN'S ORGANIZATION, WHOSE MEMBERS ARE MOTIVATED AND INSPIRED TO STRENGTHEN THEIR PARTNERSHIP WITH ISRAEL, ENSURE JEWISH CONTINUITY, AND REALIZE THEIR POTENTIAL AS A DYNAMIC FORCE IN AMERICAN SOCIETY. MEMBERS ARE FURTHER ORGANIZED INTO CHAPTERS. HADASSAH'S MEMBERS ELECT THE ELECTED DIRECTORS OF THE NATIONAL BOARD ANNUALLY AT THEIR NATIONAL BUSINESS MEETING WHILE OTHER DIRECTORS SERVE BY VIRTUE OF THEIR POSITION IN THE ORGANIZATION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7A
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SEE FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6 EXPLANATION ABOVE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 7B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: SEE FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 6 EXPLANATION ABOVE.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: REVIEW PROCESS OF FORM 990 THE RETURN IS PREPARED BY AN INDEPENDENT ACCOUNTING FIRM BASED ON INFORMATION PROVIDED BY HWZOA AND IN CONSULTATION WITH HWZOA'S STAFF. THE DRAFT PREPARED BY THE ACCOUNTING FIRM IS THEN CAREFULLY REVIEWED BY HWZOA. THE FORM 990 IS MADE AVAILABLE FOR PUBLIC INSPECTION UPON REQUEST.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY A COPY OF THE CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY IS GIVEN TO THE NATIONAL BOARD MEMBERS AND ALL NATIONAL STAFF ONCE EACH YEAR, AND IS ALSO AVAILABLE YEAR-ROUND ON OUR INTRANET AND IN THE PERSONNEL POLICY MANUAL. A CONFLICT OF INTEREST DISCLOSURE STATEMENT IS OBTAINED ANNUALLY FROM ALL NATIONAL BOARD MEMBERS AND ALL NATIONAL STAFF WHO ARE CURRENTLY SERVING THE ORGANIZATION. WHEN A CONFLICT ARISES FOR ANY NATIONAL BOARD MEMBER, THAT NATIONAL BOARD MEMBER SHALL REPORT IT IN WRITING TO THE CHAIR OF THE ETHICS COMMITTEE. WHEN A CONFLICT OF INTEREST ARISES FOR ANY NATIONAL STAFF MEMBER, THAT STAFF MEMBER SHALL REPORT IT IN WRITING TO THEIR DIRECT SUPERVISOR AND TO THE DIRECTOR OF HUMAN RESOURCES. THE POTENTIAL CONFLICT IS THEN BROUGHT TO THE CHAIR OF THE ETHICS COMMITTEE. THE ETHICS COMMITTEE REVIEWS EACH DISCLOSURE AT A MEETING CONVENED FOR THAT PURPOSE. THE INTERESTED INDIVIDUAL IS NOT PERMITTED TO VOTE ON THE RELATED MATTER, PARTICIPATE IN OR BE PRESENT AT THE DISCUSSION.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: COMPENSATION THERE ARE NO COMPENSATED OFFICERS IN THE CHAPTER STRUCTURE AND, THEREFORE, NONE ARE LISTED ON THE HADASSAH GROUP RETURN. THE NATIONAL OFFICERS OF HWZOA PROVIDE LEADERSHIP TO THE CHAPTERS. WHEN AN OFFICER OR KEY EMPLOYEE IS HIRED BY HWZOA, THE ORGANIZATION CONDUCTS A REVIEW OF VARIOUS COMPARABILITY DATA FOR COMPARABLE POSITIONS AT SIMILARLY SITUATED ORGANIZATIONS, WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF OUTSIDE COUNSEL AND SEARCH FIRM. THE ORGANIZATION SETS COMPENSATION WITHIN THE RANGE OF THE GOING MARKET RATE. THE COMPENSATION AMOUNT IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE OF THE BOARD, AND RECORDS OF THIS PROCESS ARE MAINTAINED. NO INDIVIDUAL HAVING A ONFLICT OF INTEREST IS PERMITTED TO PARTICIPATE IN THE REVIEW OR DECISION. THESE PROCEDURES ARE DOCUMENTED CONTEMPORANEOUSLY. IN SUBSEQUENT YEARS, AN INDIVIDUAL MAY RECEIVE A SALARY INCREASE AS APPROVED BY THE COMPENSATION COMMITTEE. BONUSES ARE GENERALLY NOT AWARDED.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: GOVERNING DOCUMENTS THE ORGANIZATION DOES NOT MAKE ITS GOVERNING DOCUMENTS OR CONFLICT OF INTEREST POLICY AVAILABLE TO THE PUBLIC. FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST. SMALLER CHAPTERS FINANCIAL INFORMATION: HWZOA HAS APPROXIMATELY 525 UNITS AROUND THE COUNTRY, SOME OF WHICH ARE INACTIVE OR HAVE MINIMAL ACTIVITY. INFORMATION NOT INCLUDED IN THIS FILING HAS BEEN DETERMINED TO BE DEMINIMIS/IMMATERIAL.

DisregardedEntityName.BusinessNameLine1Txt: MEIR SHFEYAH FOR PROMOTION OF EDUCATION
